scenario_title: Add RR under existing CNAME owner

group_1.behavior: Rejects the update when the add_rr would violate CNAME exclusivity, typically leaving the CNAME-only state intact.
group_1.post_state: QUERY alias.campus.edu. A returns no A rrset; QUERY alias.campus.edu. CNAME still returns target.campus.edu.

group_2.behavior: Accepts or otherwise processes the update differently, potentially replacing the CNAME or allowing an illegal mixed state.
group_2.post_state: QUERY alias.campus.edu. A returns 192.0.2.40 and CNAME may be absent or still present depending on implementation.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2181 §10.1 and §5.2.2 require that a node with a CNAME not have other data, but RFC 2136 §3.4.2 does not specify the exact UPDATE failure mode or state transition when add_rr targets such a node. Implementations may therefore disagree on whether to reject the UPDATE with YXRRSET/SERVFAIL or accept it and alter the existing CNAME state.
expected_divergence.test_rationale: The zone starts with alias.campus.edu. as a pure CNAME owner. The single UPDATE tries to add an A RR at that same owner, which is wire-valid but semantically conflicts with CNAME exclusivity. The follow-up A and CNAME queries make the outcome observable: either the server rejects the change and preserves the CNAME-only state, or it accepts the change and exposes a different post-state, including a possible illegal mixed node.
